question_title: What will be the statewide voter turnout percentage in Bavaria’s local elections (Kommunalwahlen)?
summary: Bavaria's Kommunalwahlen (local elections) have shown turnout of 59.5% (2008), 54.7% (2014, historic low), and 58.7% (2020, during COVID). For 2026, I estimate a median of ~60.5%, reflecting: (1) the post-2014 recovery trend continuing, (2) no COVID disruption unlike 2020, (3) elevated general political engagement in Bavaria (Bundestag 2025 turnout was 84.5%, Landtag 2023 was 73.1%), and (4) growing mail-in voting infrastructure. The main uncertainty is whether Kommunalwahlen-specific engagement follows the broader political engagement trend upward, or remains structurally capped at ~58-60%. With only 3 recent data points and per-cycle swings of 4-5 pp, uncertainty is substantial.

factors.description: 2020 Kommunalwahlen turnout was 58.7%, recovering from 2014 historic low of 54.7% - establishes recent baseline and upward trajectory
No COVID disruption in 2026 (2020 was held during pandemic) - should mildly boost turnout vs 2020 baseline
Very high recent political engagement in Bavaria: Bundestag 2025 at 84.5%, Landtag 2023 at 73.1% - suggests elevated civic participation
Growing Briefwahl (mail voting) infrastructure lowers barriers to participation
Kommunalwahlen are structurally 'second-order' elections with a persistent 20-25 pp gap below Bundestag/Landtag - limits upside from general engagement
Only 3 recent data points (2008, 2014, 2020) with per-cycle swings of ~4-5 pp - high inherent uncertainty

Based on historical data and contextual analysis:

**Key historical turnout figures:**
- 2008: 59.5%
- 2014: 54.7% (historic low)
- 2020: 58.7% (during COVID)

**My median estimate: ~60.5%** with a P10-P90 range of 56-65%.

The slight upward shift from 2020 reflects three main factors: (1) no pandemic disruption, (2) elevated civic engagement in Bavaria recently (84.5% turnout at 2025 Bundestag, 73.1% at 2023 Landtag), and (3) continued growth of mail-in voting. However, Kommunalwahlen structurally attract lower turnout than state/federal elections, limiting the upside. With only 3 recent data points showing 4-5 pp swings per cycle, uncertainty is substantial.
